summary.reason: Best accuracy 90.3% — in the 80-95% band, so REFINEMENT is the correct next step. 7 of 12 failures are flagged fixable; focus on the high-impact ones (T183 vernalization saturation, T127/T128 Warm-FT bypass). The 5 unfixable failures (T016 BRM missing, T077/T051/T148 opposing-mutant epistasis, T176 rescue validator limitation) should be accepted as framework / scope limitations and documented in the paper Methods.

comparison.strengths: Deterministic, closed-form, fast. 98.9% convergence. High F1 on increased (0.886) and decreased (0.929). Matches RWR on direction but with simpler math.
comparison.weaknesses: Lowest kappa (0.783) among the three; struggles with opposing-mutant epistasis (T077, T148) and FLC-saturation under vernalization (T183). Fails GI OE under warm (T092).

comparison.strengths: Sigmoidal Hill saturation handles some non-linearities better than linear geometric mean; correctly predicts GI OE under warm (T092) and PIF4 KO under warm (T127/128). Highest MCC (0.558).
comparison.weaknesses: Lowest convergence rate (65.6%) of the three. Introduces its own ODE-parameter edge-cases (T129, T166) at best (K=0.5, n=3). Only method that fails T166 (canonical tfl1 phenotype).

comparison.strengths: Perfect 100% convergence. Highest kappa (0.8015) — narrowly in almost-perfect band. Robust to parameter choice — alpha=0.6 through 0.99 all give identical accuracy. Uses signed-graph propagation rather than iterative equations.
comparison.weaknesses: Shares Warm-Temperature PIF4 failures (T127/128, T092) with Algebraic. Does not capture Hill-type saturation dynamics — same failure profile as Algebraic on epistasis.
